element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core movement foundation for entire game

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Between acts or within act transitions
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Spatial reorientation puzzle; shifts perspective entirely
Resource-gated progression; max 3 grenades normally provided
Introduces missile batteries as obstacle type

typical_relief_triggers: Safe platforming sections between combat zones
typical_tension_triggers: Limited grenade allocation requiring resource management
Nazi Base encounters with tank turrets and missile batteries
Optional areas with inaccessible content (grenade shortage)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Hybrid pacing mixing contemplative assassination setup phases with abrupt perspective-shifts to 3D navigation; tension driven by resource scarcity and environmental disorientation rather than relentless action

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Optional buildings containing side-branching corridors; Act 3 building signals deeper secrets via locked grenade-gated passages
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Inaccessible areas marked by resource shortage (requires 4 grenades, only 3 provided); down-ladders lead to hidden basement levels; Hitler's Brain Room accessible only via memory manipulation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to side-view platforming and sniper mechanics in East Berlin; safe baseline with narrative setup
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Acts 2-3 introduce perspective-shifting 3D maze segments; resource management (grenades); hotel encounters add cinematic pacing variety
difficulty_curve.late_game: Nazi Base introduces sustained combat pressure (tank turrets, missile batteries); Antarctic finale; grenade scarcity creates hard gates
notable_spikes: First 3D maze transition (disorientation from perspective shift)
Act 3 optional building (grenade gate at 3/4 allocation)
Nazi Base tank turret sequences (sustained projectile threats)

notable_level_patterns.description: Completion of act triggers dialogue/story beat (hotel rendezvous in Acts 2 and 4/5); acts separated by geographic locations (Berlin → Athens → Rio → Alexander Island)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Act 2 hotel sequence with Cherry Grace

notable_level_patterns.description: 3D first-person maze segments embedded between or within acts; requires spatial reorientation; functions as pacing interruption and cognitive load spike
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Implicit between-act transitions (specific levels not documented)

notable_level_patterns.description: Optional buildings contain side-paths locked behind grenade counts exceeding normal allocation; creates soft boundaries and hidden completion states
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Act 3 optional building with basement corridor requiring 4 grenades

notable_level_patterns.description: Nazi Base introduces sustained threat density with tank turrets and missile batteries; escalates from stealth-based assassination focus

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Golgo 13: Top Secret Episode)
